"""Client-neutral remote bridge controls for remote clients."""

import base64

from src.runtime.ids import stable_id


class RemoteBridgeRuntimeError(RuntimeError):
    """Raised when a remote bridge request is invalid or unsafe."""


REMOTE_DEVICE_TTL_DAYS = 90
MAX_REMOTE_DEVICE_TTL_DAYS = 365
REMOTE_SESSION_TTL_HOURS = 24
MAX_REMOTE_SESSION_TTL_HOURS = 168
MAX_REMOTE_ATTACHMENTS = 20
MAX_REMOTE_ATTACHMENT_BYTES = 5_000_000
MAX_REMOTE_ATTACHMENT_TOTAL_BYTES = 20_000_000
MAX_REMOTE_MESSAGE_CHARS = 100_000
MAX_REMOTE_MESSAGE_ID_CHARS = 128
MAX_REMOTE_ATTACHMENT_NAME_CHARS = 160
MAX_REMOTE_PATH_SEGMENT_CHARS = 96
MAX_REMOTE_ATTACHMENT_BASE64_CHARS = ((MAX_REMOTE_ATTACHMENT_BYTES + 2) // 3) * 4
SUPPORTED_INLINE_IMAGE_TYPES = {
    "image/gif",
    "image/jpeg",
    "image/png",
    "image/webp",
}
REMOTE_MODE_COMMANDS = {

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TrustedDeviceEnrollment:
    """One trusted remote device enrollment."""

    device_id: str
    display_name: str
    token: str
    token_hash: str
    trusted: bool
    enrolled_at: datetime
    expires_at: datetime

    def public_payload(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
        """Return a JSON-compatible payload safe to return to the enrolling client."""
        return {
            "device_id": self.device_id,
            "display_name": self.display_name,
            "trusted": self.trusted,
            "token": self.token,
            "enrolled_at": self.enrolled_at.isoformat(),
            "expires_at": self.expires_at.isoformat(),
        }

    def stored_payload(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
        """Return a JSON-compatible persisted payload without the raw token."""
        return {
            "device_id": self.device_id,
            "display_name": self.display_name,
            "trusted": self.trusted,
            "token_hash": self.token_hash,
            "enrolled_at": self.enrolled_at.isoformat(),
            "expires_at": self.expires_at.isoformat(),
        }


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RemoteSessionLink:
    """Remote-control URL and QR payload for one session."""

    link_id: str
    session_id: str
    device_id: str | None
    remote_url: str
    qr_payload: str
    bridge_token_required: bool
    allowed_commands: tuple[str, ...]
    created_at: datetime
    expires_at: datetime

    def as_json(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
        """Return a JSON-compatible remote session link."""
        return {
            "link_id": self.link_id,
            "session_id": self.session_id,
            "device_id": self.device_id,
            "remote_url": self.remote_url,
            "qr_payload": self.qr_payload,
            "qr_payload_format": "url",
            "qr_payload_metadata": {
                "session_id": self.session_id,
                "device_id": self.device_id,
                "remote_url": self.remote_url,
                "expires_at": self.expires_at.isoformat(),
                "token_header": "X-Codigote-Trusted-Device-Token",
            },
            "bridge_token_required": self.bridge_token_required,
            "allowed_commands": list(self.allowed_commands),
            "created_at": self.created_at.isoformat(),
            "expires_at": self.expires_at.isoformat(),
        }


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BridgeCommandDecision:
    """Remote command safety decision."""

    allowed: bool
    command_name: str | None
    reason: str
    risk_tier: str

    def as_json(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
        """Return a JSON-compatible command decision."""
        return {
            "allowed": self.allowed,
            "command_name": self.command_name,
            "reason": self.reason,
            "risk_tier": self.risk_tier,
        }

class RemoteAttachmentRecord:

    attachment_id: str

    def as_json(self) -> dict[str, Any]:

class _PreparedRemoteAttachment:

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class InboundBridgeMessage:
    """Normalized inbound message from a remote client."""

    message_id: str
    prompt: str
    original_prompt: str
    attachments: tuple[RemoteAttachmentRecord, ...]
    command_decision: BridgeCommandDecision
    received_at: datetime

    def as_json(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
        """Return a JSON-compatible inbound message."""
        return {
            "message_id": self.message_id,
            "prompt": self.prompt,
            "original_prompt": self.original_prompt,
            "attachments": [item.as_json() for item in self.attachments],
            "command_decision": self.command_decision.as_json(),
            "received_at": self.received_at.isoformat(),
        }


class RemoteBridgeRuntime:
    """Normalize remote bridge trust, links, messages, attachments, and commands."""

    def capabilities(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
        """Return the client-neutral remote bridge capability contract."""
        return {
            "trusted_devices": {
                "supported": True,
                "token_header": "X-Codigote-Trusted-Device-Token",
                "default_ttl_days": REMOTE_DEVICE_TTL_DAYS,
                "max_ttl_days": MAX_REMOTE_DEVICE_TTL_DAYS,
                "verify_endpoint": "/v1/sessions/{session_id}/remote/devices/verify",
                "heartbeat_endpoint": "/v1/sessions/{session_id}/remote/devices/heartbeat",
            },
            "session_links": {
                "supported": True,
                "default_ttl_hours": REMOTE_SESSION_TTL_HOURS,
                "max_ttl_hours": MAX_REMOTE_SESSION_TTL_HOURS,
                "url_shape": "/remote/sessions/{session_id}",
                "qr_payload": "remote_url",
                "qr_payload_formats": ["url", "metadata"],
                "verify_endpoint": "/v1/sessions/{session_id}/remote/session-link/verify",
            },
            "inbound_messages": {
                "supported": True,
                "history_endpoint": "/v1/sessions/{session_id}/remote/messages",
                "polling": "GET with after_cursor and limit",
                "max_message_chars": MAX_REMOTE_MESSAGE_CHARS,
                "content_shapes": [
                    "prompt",
                    "text",
                    "content",
                    "message.content",
                    "Anthropic text/image content blocks",
                    "OpenAI input_text/input_image content blocks",
                ],
                "max_message_id_chars": MAX_REMOTE_MESSAGE_ID_CHARS,
            },
            "inbound_attachments": {
                "supported": True,
                "max_attachment_bytes": MAX_REMOTE_ATTACHMENT_BYTES,
                "max_attachments": MAX_REMOTE_ATTACHMENTS,
                "max_total_attachment_bytes": MAX_REMOTE_ATTACHMENT_TOTAL_BYTES,
                "fields": ["attachments", "inline content image blocks"],
                "encoding": "base64",
                "storage": ".codigote/remote_uploads/{session_id}",
            },
            "command_filtering": {
                "supported": True,
                "safe_commands": sorted(SAFE_REMOTE_COMMANDS),
                "remote_mode_commands": sorted(REMOTE_MODE_COMMANDS),
                "bridge_safe_local_commands": sorted(BRIDGE_SAFE_LOCAL_COMMANDS),
                "blocked_commands": sorted(BLOCKED_REMOTE_COMMANDS),
            },
        }

    def enroll_trusted_device(
        self,
        *,
        display_name: str,
        trusted: bool = True,
        ttl_days: int = REMOTE_DEVICE_TTL_DAYS,
    ) -> TrustedDeviceEnrollment:
        """Create a trusted-device token enrollment."""
        normalized_display_name = display_name.strip() or "Remote device"
        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        token = secrets.token_urlsafe(32)
        device_id = stable_id(
            "remote_device",
            {
                "display_name": normalized_display_name,
                "token": token,
                "enrolled_at": now.isoformat(),
            },
        )
        return TrustedDeviceEnrollment(
            device_id=device_id,
            display_name=normalized_display_name,
            token=token,
            token_hash=self.hash_device_token(token),
            trusted=trusted,
            enrolled_at=now,
            expires_at=now + timedelta(days=_bounded_ttl(ttl_days, MAX_REMOTE_DEVICE_TTL_DAYS)),
        )

    def verify_trusted_device(
        self,
        *,
        token: str | None,
        device_records: tuple[dict[str, Any], ...],
    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
        """Verify a trusted device token against stored enrollment payloads."""
        if token is None or not token.strip():
            raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError("Trusted device token is required.")
        token_hash = self.hash_device_token(token)
        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        for record in self.latest_device_records(device_records):
            if record.get("trusted") is not True:
                continue
            stored_hash = record.get("token_hash")
            if not isinstance(stored_hash, str):
                continue
            if not hmac.compare_digest(stored_hash, token_hash):
                continue
            expires_at = _parse_datetime(str(record.get("expires_at", "")))
            if expires_at <= now:
                raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError("Trusted device token has expired.")
            return record
        raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError("Trusted device token is not recognized.")

    def revoke_trusted_device(
        self,
        *,
        device_id: str,
        device_records: tuple[dict[str, Any], ...],
    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
        """Return a revoked device payload from a stored enrollment."""
        for record in device_records:
            if record.get("device_id") == device_id:
                revoked = dict(record)
                revoked["trusted"] = False
                revoked["revoked_at"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
                return revoked
        raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError(f"Unknown trusted device: {device_id}")

    def trusted_device_heartbeat(
        self,
        *,
        token: str | None,
        device_records: tuple[dict[str, Any], ...],
        client_adapter: str | None = None,
        client_version: str | None = None,
    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
        """Verify a trusted device and return heartbeat metadata for persistence."""
        device = self.verify_trusted_device(token=token, device_records=device_records)
        seen_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        return {
            "device_id": device["device_id"],
            "trusted": True,
            "client_adapter": client_adapter,
            "client_version": client_version,
            "seen_at": seen_at.isoformat(),
            "expires_at": device.get("expires_at"),
        }

    def create_session_link(
        self,
        *,
        session_id: str,
        public_base_url: str,
        device_id: str | None = None,
        ttl_hours: int = REMOTE_SESSION_TTL_HOURS,
        allowed_commands: tuple[str, ...] = DEFAULT_ALLOWED_REMOTE_COMMANDS,
    ) -> RemoteSessionLink:
        """Create a remote-control URL and QR payload."""
        base = _public_base_url(public_base_url)
        normalized_allowed_commands = _normalize_allowed_commands(allowed_commands)
        quoted_session = quote(session_id, safe="")
        remote_url = f"{base}/remote/sessions/{quoted_session}"
        if device_id is not None:
            remote_url = f"{remote_url}?device_id={quote(device_id, safe='')}"
        created_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        expires_at = created_at + timedelta(
            hours=_bounded_ttl(ttl_hours, MAX_REMOTE_SESSION_TTL_HOURS)
        )
        link_id = stable_id(
            "remote_session_link",
            {
                "session_id": session_id,
                "device_id": device_id,
                "remote_url": remote_url,
                "created_at": created_at.isoformat(),
            },
        )
        return RemoteSessionLink(
            link_id=link_id,
            session_id=session_id,
            device_id=device_id,
            remote_url=remote_url,
            qr_payload=remote_url,
            bridge_token_required=True,
            allowed_commands=normalized_allowed_commands,
            created_at=created_at,
            expires_at=expires_at,
        )

    def verify_session_link(
        self,
        *,
        session_id: str,
        session_link_records: tuple[dict[str, Any], ...],
        device_records: tuple[dict[str, Any], ...],
        device_token: str | None = None,
        link_id: str | None = None,
        device_id: str | None = None,
    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
        """Verify that a remote session link is current and usable."""
        link = _latest_record(session_link_records) if link_id is None else None
        if link_id is not None:
            for record in session_link_records:
                if record.get("link_id") == link_id:
                    link = record
                    break
        if link is None:
            raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError("Remote session link is not recognized.")
        if link.get("session_id") != session_id:
            raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError("Remote session link does not match this session.")
        expires_at = _parse_datetime(str(link.get("expires_at", "")))
        if expires_at <= datetime.now(timezone.utc):
            raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError("Remote session link has expired.")
        bridge_token_required = link.get("bridge_token_required") is not False
        if bridge_token_required and (device_token is None or not device_token.strip()):
            raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError("Trusted device token is required for this link.")
        verified_device: dict[str, Any] | None = None
        if device_token is not None:
            verified_device = self.verify_trusted_device(
                token=device_token,
                device_records=device_records,
            )
            link_device_id = link.get("device_id")
            if isinstance(link_device_id, str) and link_device_id:
                if verified_device.get("device_id") != link_device_id:
                    raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError(
                        "Trusted device token does not match remote session link."
                    )
        if verified_device is None and device_id is not None:
            link_device_id = link.get("device_id")
            if isinstance(link_device_id, str) and link_device_id and device_id != link_device_id:
                raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError(
                    "Trusted device id does not match remote session link."
                )
        return {
            "valid": True,
            "session_id": session_id,
            "link_id": link.get("link_id"),
            "remote_url": link.get("remote_url"),
            "device_id": link.get("device_id"),
            "device_verified": verified_device is not None,
            "verified_device_id": (
                verified_device.get("device_id") if verified_device is not None else None
            ),
            "bridge_token_required": bridge_token_required,
            "expires_at": link.get("expires_at"),
            "allowed_commands": list(_safe_stored_allowed_commands(link)),
        }

    def remote_status(
        self,
        *,
        session_id: str,
        device_records: tuple[dict[str, Any], ...],
        session_link_records: tuple[dict[str, Any], ...],
    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
        """Return a remote bridge status snapshot for one session."""
        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        latest_devices = self.latest_device_records(device_records)
        active_devices = [
            item
            for item in latest_devices
            if item.get("trusted") is True
            and _parse_datetime(str(item.get("expires_at", ""))) > now
        ]
        latest_link = _latest_record(session_link_records)
        safe_link: dict[str, Any] | None = None
        if latest_link is not None:
            safe_link = dict(latest_link)
            safe_link["allowed_commands"] = list(_safe_stored_allowed_commands(safe_link))
        return {
            "session_id": session_id,
            "remote_enabled": len(active_devices) > 0,
            "trusted_device_count": len(latest_devices),
            "active_trusted_device_count": len(active_devices),
            "active_device_ids": [
                item["device_id"]
                for item in active_devices
                if isinstance(item.get("device_id"), str)
            ],
            "latest_session_link": safe_link,
            "latest_session_link_valid": (
                safe_link is not None
                and _parse_datetime(str(safe_link.get("expires_at", ""))) > now
            ),
            "capabilities": self.capabilities(),
        }

    def latest_device_records(
        self, device_records: tuple[dict[str, Any], ...]
    ) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
        """Return latest trusted-device audit payloads by device id."""
        return _latest_records_by_key(device_records, key="device_id")

    def filter_remote_command(self, text: str) -> BridgeCommandDecision:
        """Return whether a remote text command is bridge-safe."""
        stripped = text.strip()
        if not stripped:
            return BridgeCommandDecision(
                allowed=False,
                command_name=None,
                reason="message is empty",
                risk_tier="invalid",
            )
        if not stripped.startswith("/"):
            return BridgeCommandDecision(
                allowed=True,
                command_name=None,
                reason="plain user message",
                risk_tier="low",
            )
        command_name = _command_name(stripped)
        if not command_name:
            return BridgeCommandDecision(
                allowed=False,
                command_name=None,
                reason="slash command is empty",
                risk_tier="invalid",
            )
        if command_name in BLOCKED_REMOTE_COMMANDS:
            return BridgeCommandDecision(
                allowed=False,
                command_name=command_name,
                reason="command is blocked for remote bridge clients",
                risk_tier="high",
            )
        if command_name not in SAFE_REMOTE_COMMANDS:
            return BridgeCommandDecision(
                allowed=False,
                command_name=command_name,
                reason="command is not in the remote bridge allowlist",
                risk_tier="medium",
            )
        return BridgeCommandDecision(
            allowed=True,
            command_name=command_name,
            reason="command is bridge-safe",
            risk_tier="low",
        )

    def normalize_inbound_message(
        self,
        *,
        session_id: str,
        workspace_ref: str,
        payload: dict[str, Any],
    ) -> InboundBridgeMessage:
        """Normalize inbound remote text and attachments into a client message."""
        explicit_attachments = _attachment_payloads(payload)
        prompt, content_attachments = _content_from_payload(
            payload,
            allow_empty=bool(explicit_attachments),
        )
        if len(prompt) > MAX_REMOTE_MESSAGE_CHARS:
            raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError("Remote message exceeds maximum length.")
        decision = (
            BridgeCommandDecision(
                allowed=True,
                command_name=None,
                reason="attachment-only user message",
                risk_tier="low",
            )
            if not prompt and (explicit_attachments or content_attachments)
            else self.filter_remote_command(prompt)
        )
        if not decision.allowed:
            raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError(decision.reason)
        attachments = self.resolve_inbound_attachments(
            session_id=session_id,
            workspace_ref=workspace_ref,
            attachments=explicit_attachments + content_attachments,
        )
        attachment_refs = " ".join(f'@"{item.path}"' for item in attachments)
        resolved_prompt = f"{attachment_refs} {prompt}".strip() if attachment_refs else prompt
        message_id = _optional_message_id(payload)
        if message_id is None:
            message_id = stable_id(
                "remote_message",
                {
                    "session_id": session_id,
                    "prompt": prompt,
                    "attachments": tuple(item.as_json() for item in attachments),
                },
            )
        return InboundBridgeMessage(
            message_id=message_id,
            prompt=resolved_prompt,
            original_prompt=prompt,
            attachments=attachments,
            command_decision=decision,
            received_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
        )

    def resolve_inbound_attachments(
        self,
        *,
        session_id: str,
        workspace_ref: str,
        attachments: tuple[dict[str, Any], ...],
    ) -> tuple[RemoteAttachmentRecord, ...]:
        """Write base64 inbound attachments under the session workspace."""
        if not attachments:
            return ()
        prepared = _prepare_attachments(session_id=session_id, attachments=attachments)
        root = _workspace_root(workspace_ref)
        upload_dir = root / ".codigote" / "remote_uploads" / _safe_path_segment(session_id)
        upload_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        records: list[RemoteAttachmentRecord] = []
        for item in prepared:
            target = upload_dir / (
                f"{_safe_path_segment(item.attachment_id)[:16]}"
                f"-{_bytes_hash(item.data)[:12]}-{item.file_name}"
            )
            target.write_bytes(item.data)
            records.append(
                RemoteAttachmentRecord(
                    attachment_id=item.attachment_id,
                    file_name=item.file_name,
                    content_type=item.content_type,
                    path=str(target),
                    size_bytes=len(item.data),
                )
            )
        return tuple(records)

    @staticmethod
    def hash_device_token(token: str) -> str:
        """Return a stable hash for a raw trusted-device token."""
        return hashlib.sha256(token.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()


def _content_from_payload(
    payload: dict[str, Any],
    *,
    allow_empty: bool = False,
) -> tuple[str, tuple[dict[str, Any], ...]]:
    """Extract text and inline attachments from an inbound remote payload."""
    message_type = payload.get("type")
    if message_type is not None and message_type != "user":
        raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError("Inbound remote message must be a user message.")
    if "prompt" in payload:
        prompt = payload["prompt"]
    elif "text" in payload:
        prompt = payload["text"]
    elif "content" in payload:
        prompt = payload["content"]
    elif isinstance(payload.get("message"), dict) and "content" in payload["message"]:
        prompt = payload["message"]["content"]
    else:
        raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError(
            "Inbound remote message must include prompt, text, or content."
        )
    if isinstance(prompt, str):
        if not prompt.strip() and not allow_empty:
            raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError("Inbound remote message is empty.")
        return prompt.strip(), ()
    if isinstance(prompt, list):
        text_parts: list[str] = []
        inline_attachments: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
        for block in prompt:
            if (
                isinstance(block, dict)
                and block.get("type") in {"text", "input_text"}
                and isinstance(block.get("text"), str)
            ):
                text_parts.append(str(block["text"]))
            elif isinstance(block, dict) and block.get("type") == "image":
                inline_attachments.append(_image_block_attachment(block, len(inline_attachments)))
            elif isinstance(block, dict) and block.get("type") == "input_image":
                inline_attachments.append(
                    _input_image_block_attachment(block, len(inline_attachments))
                )
            else:
                raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError(
                    "Inbound remote content contains an unsupported block."
                )
        text = "\n".join(text_parts).strip()
        if text or inline_attachments:
            return text, tuple(inline_attachments)
    raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError("Inbound remote content must include text.")


def _image_block_attachment(block: dict[str, Any], index: int) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Convert an Anthropic-style base64 image block to an attachment payload."""
    source = block.get("source")
    if not isinstance(source, dict):
        raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError("Image content block must include a source object.")
    if source.get("type") != "base64":

    content_type = _image_content_type(
        source.get("media_type")
        or source.get("mediaType")
        or _detect_image_content_type_from_base64(data)
    )
    extension = _image_extension(content_type)
    attachment_id = str(
        block.get("id")
        or stable_id("remote_image", {"index": index, "data_hash": _bytes_hash(data.encode())})
    )
    return {
        "attachment_id": attachment_id,
        "file_name": f"inline-image-{index + 1}.{extension}",
        "content_type": content_type,
        "data": data,
    }

        raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError("Input image content block must include an image_url.")
    content_type, data = _data_url_image(image_url)
    extension = _image_extension(content_type)
    attachment_id = str(

        or stable_id("remote_image", {"index": index, "data_hash": _bytes_hash(data.encode())})
    )

        "attachment_id": attachment_id,
        "file_name": f"inline-image-{index + 1}.{extension}",

def _data_url_image(value: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
    """Return media type and base64 data from an inline image data URL."""
    header, separator, data = value.strip().partition(",")
    normalized_header = header.lower()
    if (
        separator != ","
        or not normalized_header.startswith("data:")
        or not normalized_header.endswith(";base64")
    ):
        raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError("Input image content block must use a base64 data URL.")
    content_type = _image_content_type(
        normalized_header.removeprefix("data:").removesuffix(";base64")

        raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError("Image content block must include base64 data.")

def _image_content_type(value: Any) -> str:
    """Return a normalized supported image content type."""
    if not isinstance(value, str):
        raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError("Image content block media type must be image/*.")
    content_type = value.strip().lower()

        raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError("Image content block media type must be image/*.")
    if content_type not in SUPPORTED_INLINE_IMAGE_TYPES:
        raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError(
            "Image content block media type must be png, jpeg, gif, or webp."

    """Extract attachment payloads from the bridge client fields."""
    value = payload.get("attachments", ())
    if value is None:
        return ()
    if not isinstance(value, list | tuple):
        raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError("attachments must be an array.")
    attachments: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    for item in value:

    if len(attachments) > MAX_REMOTE_ATTACHMENTS:

def _attachment_bytes(payload: dict[str, Any]) -> bytes:

    try:

def _content_type(value: Any) -> str:
    """Return a conservative attachment content type."""
    if not isinstance(value, str):
        return "application/octet-stream"
    normalized = value.strip().lower()
    if not normalized:
        return "application/octet-stream"
    if not re.fullmatch(r"[a-z0-9][a-z0-9.+-]*/[a-z0-9][a-z0-9.+-]*", normalized):
        return "application/octet-stream"
    return normalized[:255]


def _optional_message_id(payload: dict[str, Any]) -> str | None:
    """Return a bounded client message id when one is provided."""
    raw_message_id = payload.get("message_id")
    if raw_message_id is None or raw_message_id == "":

    if not isinstance(raw_message_id, str):
        raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError("message_id must be text.")
    message_id = raw_message_id.strip()
    if not message_id:

    if len(message_id) > MAX_REMOTE_MESSAGE_ID_CHARS:
        raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError(

def _detect_image_content_type_from_base64(data: str) -> str:
    """Detect common image media types from base64 magic bytes."""
    try:

    if prefix.startswith(b"\xff\xd8\xff"):

    if prefix.startswith((b"GIF87a", b"GIF89a")):
        return "image/gif"
    if prefix.startswith(b"RIFF") and prefix[8:12] == b"WEBP":

    if content_type == "image/gif":

    if content_type == "image/webp":

    parts = text[1:].split(maxsplit=1)

309	        """Create a trusted-device token enrollment."""

311	        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

326	            trusted=trusted,

331	    def verify_trusted_device(
332	        self,
333	        *,
334	        token: str | None,
335	        device_records: tuple[dict[str, Any], ...],
336	    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
337	        """Verify a trusted device token against stored enrollment payloads."""
338	        if token is None or not token.strip():
339	            raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError("Trusted device token is required.")
340	        token_hash = self.hash_device_token(token)
341	        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
342	        for record in self.latest_device_records(device_records):
343	            if record.get("trusted") is not True:
344	                continue
345	            stored_hash = record.get("token_hash")
346	            if not isinstance(stored_hash, str):
347	                continue
348	            if not hmac.compare_digest(stored_hash, token_hash):
349	                continue
350	            expires_at = _parse_datetime(str(record.get("expires_at", "")))
351	            if expires_at <= now:
352	                raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError("Trusted device token has expired.")
353	            return record
354	        raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError("Trusted device token is not recognized.")

356	    def revoke_trusted_device(
357	        self,
358	        *,
359	        device_id: str,
360	        device_records: tuple[dict[str, Any], ...],
361	    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
362	        """Return a revoked device payload from a stored enrollment."""
363	        for record in device_records:
364	            if record.get("device_id") == device_id:
365	                revoked = dict(record)
366	                revoked["trusted"] = False
367	                revoked["revoked_at"] = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
368	                return revoked
369	        raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError(f"Unknown trusted device: {device_id}")

432	    def verify_session_link(
433	        self,
434	        *,
435	        session_id: str,
436	        session_link_records: tuple[dict[str, Any], ...],
437	        device_records: tuple[dict[str, Any], ...],
438	        device_token: str | None = None,
439	        link_id: str | None = None,
440	        device_id: str | None = None,
441	    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
442	        """Verify that a remote session link is current and usable."""
443	        link = _latest_record(session_link_records) if link_id is None else None
444	        if link_id is not None:
445	            for record in session_link_records:
446	                if record.get("link_id") == link_id:
447	                    link = record
448	                    break
449	        if link is None:
450	            raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError("Remote session link is not recognized.")
451	        if link.get("session_id") != session_id:
452	            raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError("Remote session link does not match this session.")
453	        expires_at = _parse_datetime(str(link.get("expires_at", "")))
454	        if expires_at <= datetime.now(timezone.utc):
455	            raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError("Remote session link has expired.")
456	        bridge_token_required = link.get("bridge_token_required") is not False
457	        if bridge_token_required and (device_token is None or not device_token.strip()):
458	            raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError("Trusted device token is required for this link.")
459	        verified_device: dict[str, Any] | None = None
460	        if device_token is not None:
461	            verified_device = self.verify_trusted_device(
462	                token=device_token,
463	                device_records=device_records,
464	            )
465	            link_device_id = link.get("device_id")
466	            if isinstance(link_device_id, str) and link_device_id:
467	                if verified_device.get("device_id") != link_device_id:
468	                    raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError(
469	                        "Trusted device token does not match remote session link."
470	                    )
471	        if verified_device is None and device_id is not None:
472	            link_device_id = link.get("device_id")
473	            if isinstance(link_device_id, str) and link_device_id and device_id != link_device_id:
474	                raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError(
475	                    "Trusted device id does not match remote session link."
476	                )
477	        return {
478	            "valid": True,
479	            "session_id": session_id,
480	            "link_id": link.get("link_id"),
481	            "remote_url": link.get("remote_url"),
482	            "device_id": link.get("device_id"),
483	            "device_verified": verified_device is not None,
484	            "verified_device_id": (
485	                verified_device.get("device_id") if verified_device is not None else None
486	            ),
487	            "bridge_token_required": bridge_token_required,
488	            "expires_at": link.get("expires_at"),
489	            "allowed_commands": list(_safe_stored_allowed_commands(link)),
490	        }

537	    def filter_remote_command(self, text: str) -> BridgeCommandDecision:
538	        """Return whether a remote text command is bridge-safe."""
539	        stripped = text.strip()
540	        if not stripped:
541	            return BridgeCommandDecision(
542	                allowed=False,
543	                command_name=None,
544	                reason="message is empty",
545	                risk_tier="invalid",
546	            )
547	        if not stripped.startswith("/"):
548	            return BridgeCommandDecision(
549	                allowed=True,
550	                command_name=None,
551	                reason="plain user message",
552	                risk_tier="low",
553	            )
554	        command_name = _command_name(stripped)
555	        if not command_name:
556	            return BridgeCommandDecision(
557	                allowed=False,
558	                command_name=None,
559	                reason="slash command is empty",
560	                risk_tier="invalid",
561	            )
562	        if command_name in BLOCKED_REMOTE_COMMANDS:
563	            return BridgeCommandDecision(
564	                allowed=False,
565	                command_name=command_name,
566	                reason="command is blocked for remote bridge clients",
567	                risk_tier="high",
568	            )
569	        if command_name not in SAFE_REMOTE_COMMANDS:
570	            return BridgeCommandDecision(
571	                allowed=False,
572	                command_name=command_name,
573	                reason="command is not in the remote bridge allowlist",
574	                risk_tier="medium",
575	            )
576	        return BridgeCommandDecision(
577	            allowed=True,
578	            command_name=command_name,
579	            reason="command is bridge-safe",
580	            risk_tier="low",
581	        )

583	    def normalize_inbound_message(
584	        self,
585	        *,
586	        session_id: str,
587	        workspace_ref: str,
588	        payload: dict[str, Any],
589	    ) -> InboundBridgeMessage:
590	        """Normalize inbound remote text and attachments into a client message."""
591	        explicit_attachments = _attachment_payloads(payload)
592	        prompt, content_attachments = _content_from_payload(
593	            payload,
594	            allow_empty=bool(explicit_attachments),
595	        )
596	        if len(prompt) > MAX_REMOTE_MESSAGE_CHARS:
597	            raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError("Remote message exceeds maximum length.")
598	        decision = (
599	            BridgeCommandDecision(
600	                allowed=True,
601	                command_name=None,
602	                reason="attachment-only user message",
603	                risk_tier="low",
604	            )
605	            if not prompt and (explicit_attachments or content_attachments)
606	            else self.filter_remote_command(prompt)
607	        )
608	        if not decision.allowed:
609	            raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError(decision.reason)
610	        attachments = self.resolve_inbound_attachments(
611	            session_id=session_id,
612	            workspace_ref=workspace_ref,
613	            attachments=explicit_attachments + content_attachments,
614	        )
615	        attachment_refs = " ".join(f'@"{item.path}"' for item in attachments)
616	        resolved_prompt = f"{attachment_refs} {prompt}".strip() if attachment_refs else prompt
617	        message_id = _optional_message_id(payload)
618	        if message_id is None:
619	            message_id = stable_id(
620	                "remote_message",
621	                {
622	                    "session_id": session_id,
623	                    "prompt": prompt,
624	                    "attachments": tuple(item.as_json() for item in attachments),
625	                },
626	            )
627	        return InboundBridgeMessage(
628	            message_id=message_id,
629	            prompt=resolved_prompt,
630	            original_prompt=prompt,
631	            attachments=attachments,
632	            command_decision=decision,
633	            received_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
634	        )

636	    def resolve_inbound_attachments(
637	        self,
638	        *,
639	        session_id: str,
640	        workspace_ref: str,
641	        attachments: tuple[dict[str, Any], ...],
642	    ) -> tuple[RemoteAttachmentRecord, ...]:
643	        """Write base64 inbound attachments under the session workspace."""
644	        if not attachments:
645	            return ()
646	        prepared = _prepare_attachments(session_id=session_id, attachments=attachments)
647	        root = _workspace_root(workspace_ref)
648	        upload_dir = root / ".codigote" / "remote_uploads" / _safe_path_segment(session_id)
649	        upload_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
650	        records: list[RemoteAttachmentRecord] = []
651	        for item in prepared:
652	            target = upload_dir / (
653	                f"{_safe_path_segment(item.attachment_id)[:16]}"
654	                f"-{_bytes_hash(item.data)[:12]}-{item.file_name}"
655	            )
656	            target.write_bytes(item.data)
657	            records.append(
658	                RemoteAttachmentRecord(
659	                    attachment_id=item.attachment_id,
660	                    file_name=item.file_name,
661	                    content_type=item.content_type,
662	                    path=str(target),
663	                    size_bytes=len(item.data),
664	                )
665	            )
666	        return tuple(records)

668	    @staticmethod
669	    def hash_device_token(token: str) -> str:
670	        """Return a stable hash for a raw trusted-device token."""
671	        return hashlib.sha256(token.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()

674	def _content_from_payload(
675	    payload: dict[str, Any],
676	    *,
677	    allow_empty: bool = False,
678	) -> tuple[str, tuple[dict[str, Any], ...]]:
679	    """Extract text and inline attachments from an inbound remote payload."""
680	    message_type = payload.get("type")
681	    if message_type is not None and message_type != "user":
682	        raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError("Inbound remote message must be a user message.")
683	    if "prompt" in payload:
684	        prompt = payload["prompt"]
685	    elif "text" in payload:
686	        prompt = payload["text"]
687	    elif "content" in payload:
688	        prompt = payload["content"]
689	    elif isinstance(payload.get("message"), dict) and "content" in payload["message"]:
690	        prompt = payload["message"]["content"]
691	    else:
692	        raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError(
693	            "Inbound remote message must include prompt, text, or content."
694	        )
695	    if isinstance(prompt, str):
696	        if not prompt.strip() and not allow_empty:
697	            raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError("Inbound remote message is empty.")
698	        return prompt.strip(), ()
699	    if isinstance(prompt, list):
700	        text_parts: list[str] = []
701	        inline_attachments: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
702	        for block in prompt:
703	            if (
704	                isinstance(block, dict)
705	                and block.get("type") in {"text", "input_text"}
706	                and isinstance(block.get("text"), str)
707	            ):
708	                text_parts.append(str(block["text"]))
709	            elif isinstance(block, dict) and block.get("type") == "image":
710	                inline_attachments.append(_image_block_attachment(block, len(inline_attachments)))
711	            elif isinstance(block, dict) and block.get("type") == "input_image":
712	                inline_attachments.append(
713	                    _input_image_block_attachment(block, len(inline_attachments))
714	                )
715	            else:
716	                raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError(
717	                    "Inbound remote content contains an unsupported block."
718	                )
719	        text = "\n".join(text_parts).strip()
720	        if text or inline_attachments:
721	            return text, tuple(inline_attachments)
722	    raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError("Inbound remote content must include text.")
723	
724	
725	def _image_block_attachment(block: dict[str, Any], index: int) -> dict[str, Any]:
726	    """Convert an Anthropic-style base64 image block to an attachment payload."""
727	    source = block.get("source")
728	    if not isinstance(source, dict):
729	        raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError("Image content block must include a source object.")
730	    if source.get("type") != "base64":
731	        raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError("Only base64 image content blocks are supported.")
732	    data = source.get("data")
733	    if not isinstance(data, str) or not data:
734	        raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError("Image content block must include base64 data.")
735	    content_type = _image_content_type(
736	        source.get("media_type")
737	        or source.get("mediaType")
738	        or _detect_image_content_type_from_base64(data)
739	    )
740	    extension = _image_extension(content_type)
741	    attachment_id = str(
742	        block.get("id")
743	        or stable_id("remote_image", {"index": index, "data_hash": _bytes_hash(data.encode())})
744	    )
745	    return {
746	        "attachment_id": attachment_id,
747	        "file_name": f"inline-image-{index + 1}.{extension}",
748	        "content_type": content_type,
749	        "data": data,
750	    }
751	
752	
753	def _input_image_block_attachment(block: dict[str, Any], index: int) -> dict[str, Any]:
754	    """Convert an OpenAI-style data URL image block to an attachment payload."""
755	    image_url = block.get("image_url")
756	    if isinstance(image_url, dict):
757	        image_url = image_url.get("url")
758	    if not isinstance(image_url, str) or not image_url:
759	        raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError("Input image content block must include an image_url.")
760	    content_type, data = _data_url_image(image_url)
761	    extension = _image_extension(content_type)
762	    attachment_id = str(
763	        block.get("id")
764	        or stable_id("remote_image", {"index": index, "data_hash": _bytes_hash(data.encode())})
765	    )
766	    return {
767	        "attachment_id": attachment_id,
768	        "file_name": f"inline-image-{index + 1}.{extension}",
769	        "content_type": content_type,
770	        "data": data,
771	    }
772	
773	
774	def _data_url_image(value: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
775	    """Return media type and base64 data from an inline image data URL."""
776	    header, separator, data = value.strip().partition(",")
777	    normalized_header = header.lower()
778	    if (
779	        separator != ","
780	        or not normalized_header.startswith("data:")
781	        or not normalized_header.endswith(";base64")
782	    ):
783	        raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError("Input image content block must use a base64 data URL.")
784	    content_type = _image_content_type(
785	        normalized_header.removeprefix("data:").removesuffix(";base64")
786	    )
787	    if not data:
788	        raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError("Image content block must include base64 data.")
789	    return content_type, data
790	
791	
792	def _image_content_type(value: Any) -> str:
793	    """Return a normalized supported image content type."""
794	    if not isinstance(value, str):
795	        raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError("Image content block media type must be image/*.")
796	    content_type = value.strip().lower()
797	    if not content_type.startswith("image/"):
798	        raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError("Image content block media type must be image/*.")
799	    if content_type not in SUPPORTED_INLINE_IMAGE_TYPES:
800	        raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError(
801	            "Image content block media type must be png, jpeg, gif, or webp."
802	        )
803	    return content_type
804

806	def _attachment_payloads(payload: dict[str, Any]) -> tuple[dict[str, Any], ...]:
807	    """Extract attachment payloads from the bridge client fields."""
808	    value = payload.get("attachments", ())
809	    if value is None:
810	        return ()
811	    if not isinstance(value, list | tuple):
812	        raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError("attachments must be an array.")
813	    attachments: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
814	    for item in value:
815	        if not isinstance(item, dict):
816	            raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError("attachments must contain objects.")
817	        attachments.append(dict(item))
818	    return tuple(attachments)

821	def _prepare_attachments(
822	    *,
823	    session_id: str,
824	    attachments: tuple[dict[str, Any], ...],
825	) -> tuple[_PreparedRemoteAttachment, ...]:
826	    """Decode and validate all attachments before writing any files."""
827	    if len(attachments) > MAX_REMOTE_ATTACHMENTS:
828	        raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError(
829	            f"Remote messages support at most {MAX_REMOTE_ATTACHMENTS} attachments."
830	        )
831	    prepared: list[_PreparedRemoteAttachment] = []
832	    total_bytes = 0
833	    for item in attachments:
834	        file_name = _safe_file_name(str(item.get("file_name") or "attachment"))
835	        content_type = _content_type(item.get("content_type"))
836	        raw_data = _attachment_bytes(item)
837	        if len(raw_data) > MAX_REMOTE_ATTACHMENT_BYTES:
838	            raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError(
839	                f"Attachment exceeds {MAX_REMOTE_ATTACHMENT_BYTES} bytes."
840	            )
841	        total_bytes += len(raw_data)
842	        if total_bytes > MAX_REMOTE_ATTACHMENT_TOTAL_BYTES:
843	            raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError(
844	                f"Attachments exceed {MAX_REMOTE_ATTACHMENT_TOTAL_BYTES} total bytes."
845	            )
846	        prepared.append(
847	            _PreparedRemoteAttachment(
848	                attachment_id=_attachment_id(
849	                    session_id=session_id,
850	                    item=item,
851	                    file_name=file_name,
852	                    data=raw_data,
853	                ),
854	                file_name=file_name,
855	                content_type=content_type,
856	                data=raw_data,
857	            )
858	        )
859	    return tuple(prepared)

929	def _detect_image_content_type_from_base64(data: str) -> str:
930	    """Detect common image media types from base64 magic bytes."""
931	    try:
932	        prefix = base64.b64decode(data[:64], validate=False)
933	    except (binascii.Error, ValueError):
934	        return "image/png"
935	    if prefix.startswith(b"\x89PNG\r\n\x1a\n"):
936	        return "image/png"
937	    if prefix.startswith(b"\xff\xd8\xff"):
938	        return "image/jpeg"
939	    if prefix.startswith((b"GIF87a", b"GIF89a")):
940	        return "image/gif"
941	    if prefix.startswith(b"RIFF") and prefix[8:12] == b"WEBP":
942	        return "image/webp"
943	    return "image/png"

947	    """Return a conservative extension for a supported image media type."""

970	def _public_base_url(value: str) -> str:
971	    """Normalize a public base URL used for remote session links."""
972	    stripped = value.strip().rstrip("/")
973	    parsed = urlsplit(stripped)
974	    if parsed.scheme not in {"http", "https"} or not parsed.netloc or parsed.hostname is None:
975	        raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError(
976	            "public_base_url must be an absolute http:// or https:// URL."
977	        )
978	    if parsed.username is not None or parsed.password is not None:
979	        raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError("public_base_url must not include credentials.")
980	    try:
981	        parsed.port
982	    except ValueError as error:
983	        raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError("public_base_url includes an invalid port.") from error
984	    if parsed.query or parsed.fragment:
985	        raise RemoteBridgeRuntimeError("public_base_url must not include a query or fragment.")
986	    return urlunsplit((parsed.scheme, parsed.netloc, parsed.path.rstrip("/"), "", ""))
